alfredsnippet.snippet: This is outrageous. Where are the skilled people who come in to turn this into a reusable workflow? Where are they? This kind of behavior is never tolerated in knowledge work. You do a task one time by hand? Skills. Right away. No prompt history, no nothing. Writers, we have a special skill for writers. You are drafting outreach: skills. You are preparing for a meeting: skills, right away. Building slides: skills. Spreadsheet: skills. You are summarizing docs, Slack threads, customer calls: you right to skills. You need a PRD? Believe it or not, skills. You need a launch blog, also skills. Outline, full draft. You ask for a dashboard, a memo, an agenda, a weekly update—skills. You have to rewrite the same message for the third stakeholder? Skills. Right away. You need competitive intel, account planning, call prep, roadmap updates, time tracking, customer research, voice and tone, image generation with references—skills. You want to search, synthesize, draft, format, structure, personalize? Skills. We have the best knowledge workers in the world because of skills.
alfredsnippet.name: meme parks and rec jail
